evidence_excerpts: By the end of the lesson, learners will be able to define the Holy Spirit and recognize Him as the third person of the Trinity.

evidence_excerpts: Introduction to the "Spirit" concept. Just as wind is invisible but powerful, a spirit is a non-physical being. The learner describes the nature of a spirit using physical analogies. A spirit is invisible.; We see the effects of a spirit, like we see wind moving trees.; The Spirit is the "breath" of God.

evidence_excerpts: Defining the Holy Spirit as God’s presence in us and the "Gift and the Giver." The learner defines the Holy Spirit according to Christian teaching. The Holy Spirit is God in us.; He is our greatest gift.; He is the Spirit of truth and love.
